headlines.title: Uefa says 'integrity of game at stake' after Fifa's decision to overturn US star's red card ban
headlines.description: European football's governing body criticises Fifa's decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's automatic one-match ban following an intervention by US president Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Buckingham Palace says Prince Harry will not stay there despite his team announcing he would
headlines.description: Royal sources say the Duke of Sussex had not responded to the offer of accommodation at a Royal residence by the deadline last week.

headlines.title: Pizza Express held inquiry into Andrew's Woking claim
headlines.description: BBC Newsnight has learnt the pizza chain held an internal investigation into the former prince's assertion that he had visited its Woking branch.

headlines.title: Wegovy weight loss pill now available in UK - here's what you need to know
headlines.description: The once-a-day pill, from the makers of the Wegovy weight-loss jab, can now be bought privately in UK pharmacies.

headlines.title: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
headlines.description: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

headlines.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
headlines.description: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.

headlines.title: The dark side of the Brazilian butt lift boom
headlines.description: The aesthetics industry is worth billions but campaigners say there have been numerous cases of serious harm.

headlines.title: Farage says he's 'done no wrongdoing' after benefits from ally not declared
headlines.description: The Liberal Democrats have asked the Reform UK leader to be "straight with the British people".

headlines.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 18 killed in Kyiv region
headlines.description: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

headlines.title: How to get through World Cup-induced sleep deprivation
headlines.description: The euphoria from England's dramatic victory over Mexico might not be enough to get you through Monday.

headlines.title: Watch: England's Henderson injures wrist tripping over during match celebration
headlines.description: England midfielder Jordan Henderson suffers a wrist injury while celebrating his side's dramatic World Cup last-16 win over Mexico.

headlines.title: Watch key moments from England's 3-2 win at the Azteca
headlines.description: Jude Bellingham scores twice and Harry Kane converts a penalty as 10-man England beat Mexico 3-2 in the World Cup.

headlines.title: How Mexico's World Cup run brought joy after a year of fear
headlines.description: For Mexico's football-loving community, the World Cup has been a triumph, despite their last-16 exit. The joy has spread to Southern California, home to one of the largest Mexican communities outside Mexico.

headlines.title: England into quarter-finals - what is their path to the final?
headlines.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what England will need to do to reach the final after progressing the quarter-finals.

headlines.title: From an audience of one to 45,000: Wolf Alice's homecoming gig shows how far they've come
headlines.description: The band break out their own version of Smells Like Teen Spirit at their biggest-ever headline show.

headlines.title: What Haaland's home town did when Norway beat Brazil
headlines.description: Watch how Erling Haaland's hometown of Bryne reacted to their hero scoring both goals as Norway knocked out Brazil to advance to the quarter-final of the World Cup.

headlines.title: Three things you can do to stop EU border checks at the airport costing you
headlines.description: Queues are expected at airports this summer owing to EU's new digital border control system.

headlines.title: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
headlines.description: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

headlines.title: Why Ukrainian strikes on annexed Crimea are a blow to Putin
headlines.description: Eastern Europe correspondent Sarah Rainsford explains why Crimea is a target for Kyiv, and why the peninsula matters to Vladimir Putin.

headlines.title: UK's next heatwave begins - but how will it be different?
headlines.description: As some parts of the UK start to experience their third heatwave of the year, Simon King explains how this one could be different from the last.

headlines.title: 'Posh George': Who is aristocrat and convicted criminal at heart of Farage controversy?
headlines.description: The 32-year-old reported crypto entrepreneur, once convicted of fraud in the US, is a long-time Farage ally.

headlines.title: UK fighter jets intercept Russian plane over Norwegian Sea
headlines.description: The Ministry of Defence calls Moscow's activity "unsafe" and "unprofessional".

headlines.title: ITV sells media and entertainment arm to Sky for £1.6bn
headlines.description: The two broadcasters say the deal will create a strong rival to global streaming giants.

headlines.title: Falsely convicted man who spent £500k clearing his name is rejected for compensation
headlines.description: Brian Buckle, who was falsely convicted of sex abuse, spent more than five years in prison.

headlines.title: Australia space agency has found 'likely source' of mystery space balls
headlines.description: The agency says that they "appear to be pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle".

headlines.title: Thousands of forgotten Punjabi WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
headlines.description: Nearly 10,000 soldiers' names are being added to the official database of war dead, a cherished step for many of their descendants.

headlines.title: Benedict's Law to overhaul school allergy training
headlines.description: Benedict Blythe died after an accidental exposure to an allergen at school in 2021.

headlines.title: Partner of journalist Lyra McKee hopes her death haunts her killer
headlines.description: Sara Canning described the gunman who killed Lyra as "pathetic" and believes there will never be justice.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Who Is The Fraudster Who Paid For Farage’s Security?
headlines.description: Reform denies rules were broken over benefits provided to Nigel Farage.

headlines.title: After 250 years, do US citizens still believe in the American Dream?
headlines.description: Historian Heather Cox Richardson on what history tells us about modern America

headlines.title: Integrity of game at stake over Fifa Balogun decision - Uefa
headlines.description: Uefa says Fifa's decision not to uphold Folarin Balogun's immediate ban at the World Cup is "unprecedented, incomprehensible and unjustifiable".

headlines.title: Tottenham seal £100m move for Newcastle's Tonali
headlines.description: Tottenham complete the signing of midfielder Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United for a club-record fee which could reach £100m.

headlines.title: Henderson injures wrist in England celebrations
headlines.description: England midfielder Jordan Henderson suffered a "really bad" while celebrating his side's World Cup win over Mexico, head coach Thomas Tuchel says.

headlines.title: 'You've been trying to kill me for 23 years' - should Ronaldo start?
headlines.description: Appearing at his sixth World Cup, will Cristiano Ronaldo lift the famous trophy for the first time before he retires?

headlines.title: Who produced another iconic performance? England player ratings
headlines.description: BBC Sport England reporter Alex Howell rates how the Three Lions players performed in the last-16 win over Mexico.

headlines.title: How England's epic night at the Azteca unfolded
headlines.description: Relive all the drama from England’s 3-2 win over Mexico, as Thomas Tuchel’s team progressed to a quarter-final meeting with Norway on Saturday.
